Deep Graph Learning (DGL) has emerged as a crucial
technique across various domains. However， recent
studies have exposed vulnerabilities in DGL models,such
as susceptibility to graph injection attacks (GlAs). Existing
verifying each node independently, leading to very limited
certifying performance.In this paper,we present the first
collective certificate against GlAs,which certifies a set of

Sample-wise certificates (Bojchevski et al.， 202O; Lai et al., 2023)
essentially verify the prediction for a node one by one,assuming
that the attacker can craft a different perturbed graph each time to
However，in reality，the attacker can only produce a single
perturbed graph to simultaneously disrupt all predictions for a set

outperforming the sample-wise approach significantly when the
